name: UCA

confRecord.note: 3-2-1 ASUN (10 pts, 4-10-2 overall). 2nd in the ASUN Gold Division; clinched an ASUN Championship berth. Verified 2026 via asunsports.org official standings.
3-3-1 ASUN (10 pts, 8-7-3). 4th in the ASUN.
2-2-3 ASUN (9 pts, 4-9-4). 6th in the ASUN.
5-1-2 ASUN (6-5-6 overall). 2nd in the ASUN, behind Lipscomb.
5-2 ASUN (11-6 overall). 2nd in the ASUN in its first ASUN men's-soccer season.
Central Arkansas joined the ASUN for men's soccer in 2021; the 2020-21 season predates that (COVID-affected).

titles: ASUN Conference participants — growing D1 program

notable: USL League Two connections — regional Arkansas clubs
proPlayers.draftRank: Developing D1 — limited MLS pipeline to date

acuAlignNote: Strong alignment — UCA Kinesiology covers anatomy, exercise physiology, biomechanics, resistance training, motor control, and sport psychology. Research methods included. Clinical practicum through UAMS (University of Arkansas for Medical Sciences) network. Missing: environmental physiology and some ACU-specific professional experience hours.

gpa.note: UCA is accessible for a GPA 2.8 student with athletic profile. Exercise Science / Kinesiology is a well-resourced flagship department. Athletic scholarship is the primary lever.

fin.internationalNote: UCA has a strong international student recruiting history. D1 athletic scholarship combined with academic merit aid creates strong value. Arkansas cost of living is very low — $28k/yr budget stretches significantly further than California or Florida equivalents.

culture.vibe: Mid-sized regional university with strong school spirit. Conway is a growing suburb of Little Rock (20 min) — giving reasonable city access without big-city prices. UCA has a tight-knit campus community with strong athletic culture. Kinesiology and health sciences are flagship programs with dedicated student cohorts.
culture.thingsToDo: Little Rock (20 min) — state capital, Arkansas River Trail, River Market district, Clinton Presidential Center, live music. Heifer International Village. Pinnacle Mountain State Park hiking. Hot Springs National Park (1 hr). Ozark National Forest. Arkansas Razorbacks (SEC) games in Fayetteville.
culture.socialScene: Friendly Southern US campus culture. International students present through athletics and health sciences programs. Soccer team provides immediate social network. Less cosmopolitan than Florida or California options — but warm climate and low cost of living are compensators.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Conway/Little Rock is warm (similar summer temps to Sydney) but not a coastal city. Culture is friendly and accessible — Southern US hospitality. Not the urban Sydney-like lifestyle of Miami or LA, but affordable warm-climate D1 with strong kinesiology. Good GPA/value option.
culture.lifestyleTags: Warm, small city adjacent, Southern campus

facilityDetails.stadium: Estes Stadium — 1,500 capacity, FieldTurf, lights. On-campus facility. Good D1 mid-major standard. Conway climate allows year-round outdoor training.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 practice fields adjacent to Estes Stadium. FieldTurf and grass surfaces. Conway AR climate is warm — 9+ months outdoor training viable. Summers are hot and humid (similar to Miami).
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UCA Athletics Weight Room — 10,000 sq ft, fully equipped. Free weights, Olympic platforms, Hammer Strength machines. Shared across D1 varsity sports. Sports science integration developing.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: UCA Kinesiology department has dedicated exercise science labs — biomechanics, exercise physiology, and motor control labs accessible to student-athletes. GPS tracking (basic) implemented for soccer programme. Video analysis suite with Hudl.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UCA Athletics certified athletic trainer. Strong clinical partnership with University of Arkansas for Medical Sciences (UAMS) — one of the South's leading academic medical centres. Pre-PT students have observation placement access through UAMS network. Physical therapy clinical sites in Conway and Little Rock.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: UCA Kinesiology labs: anatomy laboratory (cadavers available through UAMS partnership), biomechanics lab (Vicon motion capture basic), exercise physiology suites (VO2max, lactate, body composition). Motor Control Lab — aligns directly with ACU BESS EXSC322 motor control unit. UAMS clinical network is the strongest pre-PT asset — graduate DPT programs at UAMS are directly accessible from UCA undergraduate pathway.
facilityDetails.extras: Conway, AR — 20 min from Little Rock state capital. Pinnacle Mountain hiking (30 min). Hot Springs (1 hr — national park + thermal spas). Ozark National Forest (2 hr). Arkansas River Trail cycling. Warm climate 9+ months. Very low cost of living — $28k/yr goes significantly further than equivalent Florida or California budget.
facilityDetails.note: UCA's competitive advantage for Olivier is the UAMS clinical network — a formal academic medical centre partnership that creates a genuine pre-DPT observation and clinical placement pathway. Combined with one of the most affordable D1 price points in the guide ($28k) and warm climate, UCA is a strong value pick for a GPA-building scenario.

housing.note: Mandatory for all freshmen during their first year; exemptions handled through a separate Freshman Residency Requirement exemption process. Verified v38 via official uca.edu/housing.

coach.name: Kyle Segebart
coach.title: Head Men's Soccer Coach
coach.profile: Head Men's Soccer Coach at UCA. Staff includes assistants Joe LaCasto and Will Montgomery, plus Director of Soccer Operations Travis Crane. International recruiting focus — 2025 roster draws from Europe (France, Portugal, Czech Republic, Russia, Italy, Spain, Denmark, Japan), North America, and Central America. Graduate student heavy squad with 67% of MFs clearing before 2027. UCA also runs a reserve/JV team for additional development minutes.

devScoresNote: v42.25 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live ucasports.com MSOC staff: HC Kyle Segebart + 2 Asst (LaCasto, Montgomery) + Dir of Soccer Ops — 3 coaches, no GK coach; support = one shared AT (Hull: M Soccer/Stunt/Off-Season). Stored Tier-1 facilityDetails: Estes Stadium (1,500, shared multi-use FieldTurf), shared 10k sq ft weight room, basic GPS, Hudl, kinesiology labs (academic). Modest ASUN mid-major environment, everything shared — sits just below the Creighton ~75 anchor; near-unchanged from prior 74.
rec: UCA is an accessible warm-climate D1 option with a genuinely strong Kinesiology programme and the UAMS clinical network for pre-PT students. Conway is not a major city but Little Rock is 20 minutes away. The $28k/yr price point is among the best in D1. ASUN Conference provides legitimate D1 credentials. Best fit for an athlete who needs affordable D1 entry with strong academic pre-PT infrastructure and a warm climate.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Outstanding opening — 6 of 9 MFs (67%) clear before Olivier arrives, including 3 graduate students. Zero current sophomores in MF means NO 2027 seniors blocking him. Coach must recruit 4-6 central midfielders in 2026+2027 classes — Olivier can position himself as the standout international recruit for those spots. Highest MF opportunity of any D1 in the guide. High recruit risk = real competition for those open spots from the portal.

name: GCU
kinRank: B.S. Kinesiology — pre-health emphasis with sport science labs
acuAlignNote: Full alignment — GCU's BS Exercise Science: Pre-Physical Therapy emphasis is purpose-built for DPT pathways. Required courses include anatomy with cadavers (BIO-210/211), exercise physiology (EXS-340), kinesiology/biomechanics (EXS-335 + lab), resistance training (EXS-250), motor control and learning (EXS-305 — standalone required course), lifespan development (PSY-357), corrective exercise (EXS-318), research methods (EXS-485), and special populations (EXS-344). Sport psychology is partially covered via health promotion (EXS-430). Only gap is no mandatory clinical practicum equivalent to EXSC398. Banner Health clinical network provides strong placement access.

gpa.note: GCU is accessible for a GPA 2.8 student with a strong athletic profile. International recruiting is active — Australian players have been on the roster under Kiefer. New coach Jamie Davies brings elite development reputation.

fin.internationalNote: GCU actively recruits international athletes and provides strong athletic scholarship packages. Online degree option available (not recommended for athlete experience). Campus cost of living in Phoenix is moderate.

confRecord.note: 3-1-3 WAC (14-4-5 overall). 3rd in the regular season, then won the final WAC Tournament as the No. 3 seed - the automatic NCAA Championship bid. Verified 2026 via the official WAC standings (Wikipedia template mirror; wacsports.com now redirects).
2-6-1 WAC (5-10-3 overall). 9th in a 10-team WAC.
2-4-2 WAC (7-7-3 overall). 7th in the WAC.
4-3-2 WAC (7-8-3 overall). 6th in a 10-team WAC.
9-2-0 WAC (15-4-1 overall). WAC regular-season champions.
7-0-0 WAC (8-2-0 overall). WAC regular-season champions, undefeated in conference (2020-21 season).

titles: 4 WAC conference titles in program history

notable: Martin Luala — 2025 R1 #28 San Diego FC
proPlayers.draftRank: Growing WAC pipeline. Sweet 16 run 2025 elevated national profile.

acRank: Regional Strong — Kinesiology with pre-health emphasis
devScoresNote: v42.25 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live gculopes.com MSOC staff: HC Jamie Davies + 2 Asst (McCall, Garrihy) + Coordinator of Operations — 3 coaches, no GK coach (shallower coaching than Akron/Denver). Stored Tier-1 facilityDetails: dedicated 6,000-seat GCU Soccer Stadium (2016, one of the best mid-major venues), 65,554 sq ft Lopes Performance Center (shared all athletics) + satellite sports med at the soccer stadium, documented GPS/Hudl/HR monitoring — elite facilities drive a high technical score. Shared S&C and thin coaching pull the average below the facility level; prior devAvg 82 over-weighted fitness at 85.

coach.name: Jamie Davies
coach.title: Head Coach (appointed December 2025)
coach.profile: Jamie Davies was appointed December 18, 2025. Previously at University of North Florida (ASUN) where he engineered one of the most dramatic turnarounds in US college soccer — inherited a 3-win team ranked 203rd nationally, finished ranked #46 nationally with two consecutive conference titles and NCAA Tournament appearances in two seasons. Prior to UNF, spent 6 seasons as UCF Associate HC and recruiting coordinator — three AAC regular season titles and four NCAA Tournaments. High-intensity pressing style, elite recruiting eye. Davies replaces George Kiefer who departed for USF.

facilities: GCU Soccer Stadium — 6,000 capacity, natural grass (opened 2016)
Lopes Performance Center — 65,554 sq ft, $500K+ S&C + sports med
Kinesiology & Sports Science labs — biomechanics, physiology
Banner Health clinical network — pre-PT placement
rec: GCU is Olivier's best warm-climate D1 option that isn't Florida. Phoenix is a major city with genuine city campus energy — the 28,000-student campus has an NBA/NFL/MLB market on its doorstep. Jamie Davies' appointment is exciting — he's one of the fastest-rising coaches in US college soccer with a proven turnaround track record. The 2025 Sweet 16 run under Kiefer elevates the program's national credibility. Kinesiology degree with Banner Health clinical placement is a legitimate pre-DPT pathway. Trade-off: WAC is below ACC/Big Ten/Big East in prestige, and Davies is new — program direction is uncertain in year 1.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: New coach will reshape the roster — recruiting risk assessment reflects this coaching transition.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Davies' rebuild means 2026 recruiting class is entirely his own vision. Roster reset under new coach creates opportunity but also uncertainty. GCU has history of signing international players — Olivier profile fits the recruitment pattern.

culture.vibe: GCU is a large, fast-growing Christian university with exceptional facilities and a genuinely electric campus atmosphere. The 28,000-student residential campus has one of the most engaged student fanbases in the WAC. Phoenix provides world-class warmth, a major sports market (Suns, Cardinals, Coyotes, D-backs, Arizona FC MLS) and a diverse international community.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Phoenix is warm 10 months of the year — as close to Sydney weather as you'll find in the US. Major city with beach-less but outdoor lifestyle (Camelback Mountain, South Mountain, Papago Park). GCU's Christian culture is genuine but not restrictive — international students are well integrated. Football, soccer, and basketball games are sold-out events. Strong Australian sporting culture crossover in Phoenix.
culture.socialScene: Vibrant campus with strong club and intramural culture. Phoenix city life — restaurants, rooftop bars, Old Town Scottsdale, ASU Tempe nearby. International student community is large. Soccer team provides immediate social network.
culture.thingsToDo: Phoenix — Camelback Mountain hiking, Chase Field (D-backs), Footprint Center (Suns), State Farm Stadium (Cardinals), Arizona FC (MLS), Scottsdale nightlife, Sedona (2hr), Grand Canyon (4hr), Desert Botanical Garden. Warm 10+ months. Golf culture world-class.
culture.lifestyleTags: Warm major city, large campus, Christian culture, outdoor lifestyle, sports-mad

facilityDetails.stadium: GCU Soccer Stadium — 6,000 capacity, natural grass, dedicated soccer venue opened 2016. Chairback seats, academic wing under stadium. One of the best mid-major soccer facilities in the US.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Multiple natural grass training fields on campus. Year-round outdoor training — no weather limitations.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Lopes Performance Center — 65,554 sq ft, $500,000+ state-of-the-art equipment. Primary S&C hub for all GCU athletics. Satellite sports medicine facility located directly at GCU Soccer Stadium.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking, video analysis (Hudl), heart rate monitoring under Kiefer era. Davies expected to maintain elite sports science infrastructure.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology labs include biomechanics, exercise physiology, and motor control equipment. Banner Health network provides clinical placement for pre-PT students — major hospital system in Phoenix metro.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Lopes Sports Medicine — full-time staff with satellite facility at GCU Soccer Stadium. Primary sports med hub in Lopes Performance Center.
facilityDetails.note: GCU Stadium is one of the best dedicated soccer venues in mid-major D1 — 6,000 cap, natural grass, on-campus. The Lopes Performance Center at 65,554 sqft with satellite sports med at the stadium pushes this firmly into Excellent territory.

housing.note: Not mandatory for freshmen, but GCU has invested heavily in on-campus capacity (3,250 apartment suites + 1,557 residence hall suites as of Feb 2026). Verified v38 via official gcu.edu/why-gcu/campus-life/housing.

name: Akron
kinRank: B.S. Exercise Science — one of the strongest pre-PT pipelines in the MAC
acuAlignNote: Strong alignment — Akron Exercise Science covers anatomy, exercise physiology, biomechanics, motor control, resistance training, sport psychology, and research methods. Explicit Pre-PT concentration available. Cleveland Clinic and Summa Health System provide clinical placement — among the best pre-PT clinical networks in the Midwest.
soccerLevel: MAC Conference — D1 elite program, historically top-10 nationally
prePT: Excellent — Cleveland Clinic + Summa Health clinical network

gpa.note: Akron is one of the most accessible D1 programs academically in the guide. GPA 2.8 is well above the minimum and qualifies for merit aid. Strong international recruiting history.

fin.internationalNote: Akron has strong scholarship packages for international athletes. Public university pricing is very competitive — one of the best value D1 options in the guide. Ohio cost of living is moderate.

confRecord.note: MAC Regular Season and Tournament Champions. NCAA Tournament — continued Embick dynasty.
NCAA Tournament. MAC Championship Final.
MAC Tournament Champions. NCAA Tournament Sweet 16.
NCAA Tournament. Strong MAC showing.
MAC Tournament Champions. NCAA Tournament College Cup (Final Four).
COVID-shortened season.

notable: Emil Jaaskelainen (#7 overall 2024 MLS Draft — Inter Miami)
18 MLS picks under Coppinger era alone
proPlayers.draftRank: Top-15 all-time MLS producers. Consistent first-round pipeline.

acRank: Strong — Exercise Science with explicit Pre-PT concentration
devScoresNote: v42.25 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live gozips.com MSOC staff: HC Jared Embick + Assoc HC Coppinger + 2 Asst (Nanchoff [ex-Akron/MLS], Schmidt) + a General Manager — deep, decorated full-time coaching. Sports Performance is a shared 3-person department (Sahlfeld/Walker/Poptic) across all athletics, NO soccer-dedicated S&C or sports scientist; Sports Med does list a dedicated MSOC assistant trainer (Draper) + dietician. Stored Tier-1 facilityDetails: FirstEnergy Soccer Complex — dedicated 5,800-seat soccer-specific stadium on a 23-acre complex + indoor winter facility. 2010 national title + MLS pipeline are results, excluded — prior devAvg 85 had smuggled them in; fitness trimmed to reflect shared (not dedicated) S&C.

coach.name: Jared Embick
coach.title: Head Coach (contract extended through 2035)
coach.profile: Jared Embick has built Akron into one of the most consistent soccer programs in D1 over a decade-plus tenure. Contract extended through 2035 — a sign of total institutional commitment. Under his staff (with associate HC Ger Coppinger), Akron has won multiple MAC titles, reached the 2021 College Cup Final Four, and sent 18 MLS draft picks in Coppinger's tenure alone including Emil Jaaskelainen (#7 overall, Inter Miami, 2024). Staff: Ger Coppinger (Associate HC, 11th season), Michael Nanchoff (Asst), Josh Kay (Asst). USSF A License. Technical, high-intensity playing style.

facilities: FirstEnergy Stadium — training and home games
Akron Exercise Science Labs — biomechanics, physiology
Cleveland Clinic + Summa Health clinical network
rec: Akron is the hidden gem of this guide. The combination of a top-20 MLS pipeline, explicit Pre-PT concentration, and Cleveland Clinic clinical access is genuinely exceptional — PBA-level PT pathway quality at a public university price. Jared Embick has a contract through 2035, giving Olivier certainty about coaching continuity. The trade-off is clear: Akron, Ohio has cold winters and a rust-belt city feel — it's the furthest from the Sydney lifestyle in the guide. But for a player who prioritises soccer development, PT career pathway, and value, Akron competes with anyone.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: Elite program with high international recruiting competition.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Akron's MLS pipeline means roster competition is genuine. However, MAC quality is accessible for an 8/10 international CM — Embick regularly develops international players into starters. Years 1-2 rotation realistic if Olivier impresses in preseason.

culture.vibe: Mid-sized public university with a genuine soccer culture — Akron is known in college soccer circles as a program that takes the sport seriously. The Akron/Cleveland metro is a proud rust-belt sports market (Browns, Cavaliers, Guardians, Cleveland FC). Campus itself is urban and walkable in central Akron.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Akron is cold in winter — this is the primary drawback for a Sydney player. But the soccer development environment, Cleveland Clinic PT pathway, and team culture are exceptional. For Olivier, this is a place to develop fast, graduate with an elite PT credential, and enter the professional pipeline. Not a lifestyle-first choice — a career-first choice.
culture.socialScene: Urban campus with college-town energy. Cleveland (45 min) — Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, Progressive Field, Rocket Mortgage Fieldhouse, Cleveland FC (MLS starting 2026). Akron has a craft beer culture, vibrant arts scene. Soccer team is the prestige sport on campus.
culture.thingsToDo: Cleveland (45 min) — Rock Hall, Cleveland Museum of Art, Cuyahoga Valley National Park, Lake Erie beaches. Akron — Cuyahoga Valley bike trails, Inventure Place, Summit County Metro Parks. Columbus (2hrs), Pittsburgh (2hrs). Cold winters but excellent fall soccer weather.
culture.lifestyleTags: Cold winters, urban campus, elite soccer culture, Cleveland market, PT pathway first-class

facilityDetails.stadium: FirstEnergy Soccer Complex — ~5,800 capacity dedicated soccer facility, 23-acre complex. Natural grass. Renovated 2010 with new grandstand, multipurpose press box/suite, concessions. One of the best MAC soccer venues.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Multiple natural grass training pitches on the 23-acre complex adjacent to the stadium. Indoor facility for winter training.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Dedicated soccer S&C program — part of Akron Athletics performance infrastructure. Full Olympic weight room, speed development track.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking, video analysis, sports psychology support. Embick runs a data-informed program — one of the more analytically advanced MAC programs.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs — biomechanics, physiology, motor control equipment. Cleveland Clinic partnership is the crown jewel — one of the top 5 hospital systems in the US. Pre-PT students have direct clinical observation and placement pathways.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Full-time AT and sports medicine staff. Cleveland Clinic proximity means elite medical support available.
facilityDetails.note: 5,800 cap dedicated soccer stadium in a purpose-built 23-acre complex is strong for MAC level. Cleveland Clinic proximity elevates the overall athletic support environment.

housing.note: Mandatory for first-year freshmen from outside the 5 nearby Ohio counties (space-permitting); exemptions available for military, married, or local-family circumstances. Verified v38 via official uakron.edu/reslife.

name: Denver
kinRank: B.S. Kinesiology and Sport Studies — launched 2023, NSCA-certified preparation
acuAlignNote: Strong alignment — Denver Kinesiology covers anatomy, exercise physiology, biomechanics, motor control, sport psychology, and research methods. NSCA certification preparation integrated. Note: degree launched 2023 — newer program with growing infrastructure. Clinical placement developing through Swedish Medical Center and Denver Health.
soccerLevel: Summit League (2025), moving to West Coast Conference (2026) — D1 elite
prePT: Good — Swedish Medical Center clinical network

gpa.note: DU is selective (private university). GPA 2.8 is at the minimum threshold — improving trajectory helps. Strong athletic scholarship packages available. Small private university means personal coach attention.

fin.internationalNote: DU is a private university with a strong merit aid program. Athletic + academic scholarships can be stacked. Denver cost of living is moderate-high. International students well-supported — DU has a large international student community (~15%).

confRecord.note: Summit League Champions (9th title). NCAA Tournament Round of 32.
Summit League Champions. NCAA College Cup (Final Four). Best season in program history.
Summit League Champions. NCAA Tournament Round of 32.
Summit League Champions. NCAA Tournament Round of 32.
Summit League Champions. NCAA Tournament Round of 16.
Summit League competition. COVID-shortened season.

proPlayers.draftRank: Strong mid-major pipeline. Moving to WCC 2026 will elevate national profile.

acRank: Strong — new kinesiology program (2023) with NSCA certification pathway
devScoresNote: v42.25 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live denverpioneers.com MSOC staff: HC Jamie Franks (endowed Weinberg chair, ex-Wake Forest technical pedigree) + Assoc HC Brad Franks + Asst Arenas + a dedicated Goalkeeper Coach (Lissek) — 4 full-time incl position-specific coaching. Stored Tier-1 facilityDetails: dedicated CIBER Field soccer stadium (2009) + indoor turf + a purpose-built 10,000 sq ft S&C facility built UNDER the stadium seating (a genuinely dedicated soccer S&C space, above the shared norm) + GPS/video. Strong environment; prior devAvg 84 was partly results-driven.

coach.name: Jamie Franks
coach.title: Head Coach (10th season)
coach.profile: Jamie Franks is in his 10th season and has built Denver into the dominant Summit League program — 5 consecutive conference titles 2021–2025, 15 straight NCAA Tournaments, College Cup (Final Four) in 2024. Moving the program to the West Coast Conference in 2026 — a significant elevation in competition and national visibility. Strong recruiting eye for technical midfielders. Cole Bassett (USMNT) is his most notable development success. Franks is one of the most underrated coaches in D1 soccer.

facilities: University of Denver Soccer Stadium — 2,000 capacity, natural grass (opened 2009)
Daniel L. Ritchie Center — Olympic-grade athletic facility
rec: Denver is a sleeper pick in this guide. Five consecutive Summit League titles, a College Cup run in 2024, and moving to the West Coast Conference in 2026 — this program is on a genuine upward trajectory. Jamie Franks is one of the best under-the-radar coaches in D1. Denver city is a major US city with genuine lifestyle appeal (1 mile high, outdoor culture, beer scene, MLS team). The Kinesiology degree launched in 2023 — newer program but NSCA-certified and developing clinical links. Trade-off: Colorado winters, high cost (~$62k), and squad depth is competitive for a program this successful.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: Elite program — WCC move elevates competition.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: DU's move to WCC in 2026 raises the program's ambition and recruiting level. Olivier would enter a program in transition to a higher conference — opportunity if he earns trust early, but competition will intensify.

culture.vibe: Small private university (~12,000 students) with a genuinely excellent academic reputation and exceptional athletics culture. DU is one of the few schools where soccer is the flagship sport on campus — the program gets sold-out crowds and genuine campus-wide attention. Denver city is world-class: outdoor culture, 300 days of sunshine, MLS Colorado Rapids, Nuggets, Broncos, Rockies.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Denver city is excellent — cosmopolitan, diverse, outdoor-focused. Not warm in winter (cold Oct-March) but the rest of the year is exceptional, with 300 days of sunshine. The Mile High City has a Sydney-adjacent energy — outdoor lifestyle, cafe culture, sport culture. If climate is less important than city quality, Denver scores very high. DU's small campus means personal development.
culture.socialScene: Small DU campus with strong international community (~15% international students). Denver city life — LoDo (Lower Downtown), River North Art District, Larimer Square, Red Rocks Amphitheater, Colorado Rockies games. Colorado Rapids (MLS) proximity. Mountain access — skiing (1hr), hiking, biking.
culture.thingsToDo: Denver — Red Rocks Amphitheater, Rocky Mountain National Park (2hrs), skiing (Vail, Breckenridge, Copper — 1-2hrs), Colorado Rapids MLS, Coors Field (Rockies), Ball Arena (Nuggets), LoDo entertainment district. Outdoor lifestyle year-round despite cold winters.
culture.lifestyleTags: Major city, outdoor lifestyle, cold winters, small campus, elite soccer culture

facilityDetails.stadium: CIBER Field at University of Denver Soccer Stadium — 2,000 capacity, natural grass, dedicated soccer venue opened 2009. 10,000 sq ft strength and conditioning facility built directly under the stadium seating.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Multiple natural grass training fields. Indoor turf facility for winter training.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: 10,000 sq ft S&C facility integrated under the stadium seating — dedicated and purpose-built for soccer. Daniel L. Ritchie Center also available for multi-sport training.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking, video analysis, sports psychology. Franks runs a technically sophisticated program — analytics integrated into training.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: New Kinesiology labs (post-2023) — biomechanics, exercise physiology. Swedish Medical Center network developing for clinical placements. NSCA certification preparation integrated into curriculum.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Full-time AT staff. DU athletics has strong sports medicine infrastructure.
facilityDetails.note: Dedicated 2,000 cap soccer stadium with 10,000 sqft S&C built under the seating is an unusually integrated facility — that infrastructure in a purpose-built soccer context earns Very Good despite the modest capacity.

housing.note: Mandatory 2-year live-on requirement for first- and second-year undergraduates, including transfers; exemptions require documentation. Verified v38 via official studentaffairs.du.edu/housing.

name: Vermont
kinRank: B.S. Kinesiology — solid mid-tier program with pre-PT options
acuAlignNote: Strong alignment — UVM Kinesiology covers anatomy, exercise physiology, biomechanics, sport psychology, and research methods. UVM Medical Center provides clinical access. Reasonable pre-PT pathway.
soccerLevel: America East Conference — D1, 2024 NCAA National Champions

gpa.note: Selective public university. Athletic scholarship available. Adrian Dubois was appointed head coach for 2026 after Rob Dow left for Penn State following the 2025 season.

fin.internationalNote: Out-of-state + international tuition is high at Vermont (~$66k/yr 2025-26). Athletic scholarship reduces this significantly. New head coach for 2026 (Dubois, succeeding Rob Dow) — scholarship packages may shift under the new staff.

confRecord.note: America East Champions (beat Bryant 2-0 in final). 14-1-5 overall; NCAA Tournament 1st round (L 3-2 OT vs Hofstra). Rob Dow's final season before leaving for Penn State.
2024 NCAA National Champions — beat Marshall 2-1 OT in final. First title in any UVM sport outside skiing.
NCAA Tournament appearance.
NCAA Tournament Elite Eight.
NCAA Tournament appearance.
COVID-shortened season.

notable: Maximilian Kissel (2025 MLS Draft — championship game hero)
proPlayers.draftRank: Growing America East pipeline. 2024 championship elevated national profile significantly.

acRank: Good — UVM Medical Center pre-PT access
devScoresNote: v42.25 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live uvmathletics.com MSOC staff: HC Adrian Dubois + Assoc HC Brent + 2 Asst (Twomey, Vargas) + Dir of Ops — a deep 4-coach staff (no GK coach); support = shared AT (Tremel: M Soccer/Skiing) + a shared Assistant Athletic Performance Coach (Fleischman). Stored Tier-1 facilityDetails: Virtue Field — dedicated 2,600 natural-grass venue, indoor facility essential for Vermont winters, no GPS listed. Solid America East environment; near-unchanged from prior 73 (fitness trimmed from 78 to reflect shared, not dedicated, performance staff).

coach.name: Adrian Dubois
coach.title: Head Coach (2026, 1st season)
coach.profile: Adrian Dubois was appointed Vermont's head coach ahead of the 2026 season, succeeding Rob Dow, who left for Penn State in December 2025 after nine seasons at Vermont. Dow's tenure delivered the 2024 NCAA National Championship — the first title in any UVM sport outside skiing — and a second straight America East title in his final 2025 season (14-1-5). Dubois inherits an elite-level program but has yet to coach a game and faces heavy graduation losses from the championship era.

staff.name: Adrian Dubois
staff.bg: Appointed ahead of the 2026 season after Rob Dow left for Penn State; has yet to coach his first Vermont game.

staff.name: Travis Brent
staff.bg: Associate head coach on Adrian Dubois's staff.

staff.name: Rory Twomey
staff.bg: Assistant coach retained across the coaching transition — was on staff for the 2024 national championship.

staff.name: Eric Bissell
staff.bg: Director of operations; previously a team student manager during the 2024 championship season.

rec: Vermont is in the guide primarily due to the 2024 NCAA Championship and strong America East soccer tradition. However, Burlington VT is cold (long harsh winters), not a major city, and the lifestyle match for a Sydney-raised player is poor. Rob Dow has departed for Penn State after the 2025 season; Adrian Dubois takes over for 2026, inheriting an elite program (back-to-back America East titles, 2024 national champions) but with major graduation losses and no games coached yet. Listed depth only — worth monitoring if Olivier's profile is developing and the new coach has Australian connections, but not a priority target.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 of 11 MF (3 Gr + R-Jr + Jr) clear before 2027. Loebus + Zellefrow transferred to UNC; net 2 rising jr, 2 rising sr remaining.

culture.vibe: Small New England university with exceptional natural beauty and a campus that rallied behind its soccer team to produce a historic national championship. Burlington is a progressive, outdoorsy college town on Lake Champlain.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Burlington VT is cold — very cold. Winters are long and harsh, and the landscape is beautiful but not Sydney-like. New coach, championship squad graduated. Not a lifestyle fit but the soccer tradition is now elite.
culture.socialScene: Small college town. Burlington has good food, music, and Vermont Green FC (USL League One) proximity.
culture.thingsToDo: Burlington — Church Street Marketplace, Lake Champlain, Ben & Jerry's factory tour, Green Mountain hiking. Montreal (2hrs), Boston (3hrs). Skiing world-class (Stowe, Sugarbush). Cold winters.
culture.lifestyleTags: Cold, small city, outdoorsy, elite soccer tradition, new coach era

facilityDetails.stadium: Virtue Field — 2,600 capacity, natural grass. Famous for passionate home crowds during 2024 championship run.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: On-campus training fields. Indoor facility essential for Vermont winters.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Standard D1 S&C facility. Good quality for America East level.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Developing — Dow built a strong culture around fitness and tactical discipline.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: UVM Kinesiology labs. UVM Medical Center proximity for clinical placement.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Full-time AT staff. UVM Medical Center for advanced care.

housing.note: Mandatory for first-time first-years and required through sophomore year; UVM regularly admits more students than it can house, so forced-triple assignments are common. Verified v38 via official uvm.edu/housingdining.

name: Mercyhurst

confRecord.note: Second D1 season. 8th of 10 (3-5-1, 10 pts; 3-10-4 overall) — a sharp fall from the 2024 title. Entered ranked 1st in the NEC preseason poll.

FIRST D1 season — and NEC Division I regular-season champions (5-1-2, 17 pts). As a provisional D1 member Mercyhurst competes at D1 level but is not eligible for NCAA championship postseason until 2028–29.
D2 PSAC West, 7-2-1 — behind Gannon (9-0-1). Final full D2 season.
D2 PSAC West champions with a perfect 10-0 conference record (17-0-1 overall); top tournament seed.
D2 PSAC West champions (7-1-2, level on record with Gannon); top tournament seed.

titles: 2024 NEC Division I regular-season champions — won it in their FIRST D1 season (5-1-2, 17 pts)
2022 PSAC West champions — perfect 10-0 conference record (17-0-1 overall)
2021 PSAC West champions (7-1-2) — top tournament seed
Reclassified D2 (PSAC) → D1 (NEC), effective Fall 2024; provisional D1 status until 2028–29
Laker identity — strong Catholic liberal arts tradition

notable: Newly D1 (Fall 2024) — professional pipeline still developing
Won the NEC regular-season title in its first D1 season; strong PSAC D2 pedigree (back-to-back West titles 2021–22)
proPlayers.draftRank: N/A — provisional D1 member, no NCAA postseason until 2028–29

acuAlignNote: Strong coverage of anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise testing, and clinical internship. School of Health Professions provides direct Pre-DPT advising. Missing some ACU elective units.
soccerLevel: D1 — NEC. Won the NEC regular-season title in its first D1 season (2024). Provisional D1 until 2028–29.

gpa.note: Olivier at 2.8 meets D1 eligibility. Mercyhurst values holistic admission — athletic profile weighted.

fin.internationalNote: Mercyhurst has recruited Australians before (Ryan Mrvcic, Jared Rimmer — both from Sydney). International pipeline exists. Athletic scholarship + merit aid combination is the key lever. Total sticker $63,779 — need meaningful aid to hit budget.

culture.vibe: Small Catholic liberal arts university on the shores of Lake Erie. Tight-knit campus with strong school spirit despite being new to D1. Athletics are a centrepiece of campus life. Mercyhurst competes in multiple D1 sports and the campus atmosphere reflects that ambition.
culture.thingsToDo: Presque Isle State Park (beach/kayaking on Lake Erie), Erie downtown waterfront, Tom Ridge Environmental Center, Waldameer Park, Erie Art Museum. Pittsburgh is 2 hrs south — major city option for weekends.
culture.socialScene: Strong campus community given size. International athletes are well-integrated. Laker pride is real — new D1 status has energised the campus. Catholic culture means active service and community engagement programs.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Erie is cold (heavy lake-effect snow). Not warm-climate. But the Australian connection (Mrvcic/Rimmer from Sydney) is a genuine bridge. Small campus means Olivier would be known and valued quickly. Best fit if D1 soccer + Pre-PT degree outweighs climate.
culture.lifestyleTags: Small campus, Lake Erie, cold climate, Australian connection

facilityDetails.stadium: Mercyhurst Soccer Complex — natural grass pitch, 500-seat capacity, team dugouts, scoreboard. Upgraded as part of D1 reclassification investment.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated practice fields adjacent to the main stadium. Indoor training facility available for winter months — critical in Erie's lake-effect snow climate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Hammermill Center — main athletic weight room shared across all D1 programs. Expanded as part of NEC reclassification. Olympic lifting platforms, cable systems, cardio equipment.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: School of Health Professions — dedicated Exercise Science labs including metabolic testing, body composition analysis, and biomechanics lab. Students assist with athlete performance testing as part of curriculum.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Certified Athletic Trainers assigned to all D1 programs. Sports Medicine suite within athletic complex. UPMC partnership for specialist referrals and imaging (Pittsburgh-based network).
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science department has dedicated anatomy, physiology, and kinesiology labs. Clinical internship (500 hours) embedded in the degree — partnered with UPMC Health System and local rehabilitation clinics. Direct Pre-DPT advising from Health Professions faculty.
facilityDetails.extras: Lake Erie is a 10-minute walk from campus. Presque Isle State Park (1,600 acres) is an exceptional outdoor training and recreation environment. Ice hockey program (HE) also D1 — Mercyhurst is known as a serious multi-sport D1 program.
facilityDetails.note: New D1 investment is evident — Mercyhurst upgraded facilities as part of NEC reclassification. Not a Power 4 complex but well-resourced for the level. The Pre-DPT pipeline through UPMC is a genuine career pathway advantage.

housing.note: Mandatory for all undergraduates throughout enrollment, including first-years; exemptions limited to local Erie County commuters living with a parent/guardian, marriage, or age 24+. Verified v38 via official mercyhurst.edu/residence-life.

coach.name: Austin Solomon
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Austin Solomon has been Mercyhurst's men's head coach since 2023, and led the Lakers to the NEC regular-season title in the program's first D1 season (2024) — his staff was named NEC Coaching Staff of the Year, and Dylan Sumner was NEC Player of the Year. A fall to 8th followed in 2025. Staff includes Andrew Bennett (Assistant Coach), Logan McFadden (Player Development), and Ethan Kinyo (Goalkeeper Coach). The program has recruited internationally including two Australians from Sydney — Ryan Mrvcic (Jr.) and Jared Rimmer (Sr., departing). Solomon is building a competitive NEC identity from a strong D2 PSAC foundation.

devScoresNote: v42.25 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live hurstathletics.com MSOC staff: HC Austin Solomon + Asst Bennett + Asst/Player-Development (McFadden) + a GK Coach (Kinyo) — 4 coaches incl position-specific, decent for the level; support lists only an Equipment Manager (no soccer AT or S&C). Stored Tier-1 facilityDetails: small dedicated 500-seat Mercyhurst Soccer Complex + 2 practice fields + indoor, shared Hammermill Center weight room, Exercise Science academic labs (students assist), no GPS/video. Genuinely modest former-D2 program reclassifying to D1/NEC — D1 has no floor. Reclassification status and results excluded; prior devAvg 70 over-rated the environment.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Now in its D1 era (NEC since Fall 2024), Mercyhurst is actively expanding roster quality after an 8th-place 2025 followed the title-winning 2024. Ryan Mrvcic (Sydney, Jr.) continues through Yr 1 of Olivier's arrival. Established D1 MF depth limits immediate minutes — trajectory improves as the program settles. NEC is a competitive but not elite D1 conference. Year 3–4 opportunity is real for a quality CM.

rec: Mercyhurst offers Olivier a genuine D1 pathway in the Northeast Conference, and the programme is better than its Fit Score suggests: back-to-back PSAC West titles (2021–22, including a perfect 10-0 conference season) and then the NEC regular-season title in its very first D1 season (2024), before falling to 8th in 2025. IMPORTANT: Mercyhurst is a provisional D1 member until 2028–29 and cannot compete in the NCAA championship postseason before then — Olivier's first season (2027–28) would fall inside that window. The Exercise Science degree through the School of Health Professions provides a solid Pre-DPT foundation with 500hr clinical placement. The Australian connection (Mrvcic + Rimmer from Sydney) is a real bridge. Erie is cold and the total cost of $63,779 exceeds budget — athletic aid is essential. Best positioned as a reach D1 option where the soccer level justifies the climate compromise.

name: Delaware
kinRank: Good (Kinesiology and Applied Physiology, dedicated department)
acuAlignNote: Strong kinesiology and applied physiology — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise science well-covered. Pre-PT pathway supported.

fin.internationalNote: Delaware out-of-state COA is ~$38k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 40–60% of COA. Merit aid available for international students with 3.0+ GPA. Newark, DE has moderate cost of living. Strong academic reputation and health science programs.

gpa.note: Public university with strong academics. Athletic scholarship pathway established.

confRecord.note: 3-1-2 Summit League (11 pts, 12-2-3 overall). 3rd in Delaware's first Summit League men's-soccer season; reached the Summit League Tournament semifinal. Verified 2026 via thesummitleague.org official standings.
2-6 CAA (6 pts, 7-7-2 overall). 10th in Delaware's final CAA season before the 2025 move to the Summit League.
3-2-3 CAA (12 pts, 9-4-6 overall). 4th in the CAA.
2-6-1 CAA (7 pts, 4-11-2 overall). Last in the CAA.
0-8 CAA (2-12-1 overall). Winless in CAA play; last in the conference.
1-1-2 CAA (2-1-2 overall). 3rd in the CAA North Division (2020-21 season).

devScoresNote: v42.21 §5a re-baseline (environment only). Live bluehens.com MSOC staff: deepest CAA staff — HC McMenemy + Associate HC + 2 assts + Director of Soccer Operations + dedicated Athletic Trainer. Whitney Athletic Center 10,000-sqft S&C + nutrition + sports med; on-campus dedicated Grant Stadium. Strong large-public environment; no documented GPS/video.

coach.name: Tommy McMenemy
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Delaware is a consistently competitive CAA program — strong kinesiology and applied physiology degree. Low cost ($38k out-of-state) with accessible GPA. Newark is a classic college town with Philly 25 min south. Note: Delaware's July 2025 all-sports move to Conference USA does NOT include men's soccer, which competes in The Summit League (CUSA does not sponsor men's soccer) — the Blue Hens reached the 2025 Summit League semifinals in their first season.

rec: Strong kinesiology degree. Consistent mid-major D1 soccer (CAA through 2024, Summit League from 2025). Low cost. Newark is a smaller college town — limited lifestyle.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Small 4-player MF pool — 2 clear, 2 remain (both So). Only 2 competitors in Year 1, suggesting real starting potential for a quality CMF like Olivier.

facilityDetails.stadium: Stuart & Suzanne Grant Stadium — 1,400 capacity, on-campus dedicated soccer complex in Newark, DE.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Delaware Athletic Complex. Full D1 training infrastructure on main campus.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Whitney Athletic Center — 10,000 sq ft S&C area, large training room with sports medicine facilities, kitchen and nutrition education space, auditorium. Built into Delaware Stadium (west side). Strong D1-level infrastructure.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology and Applied Physiology labs — strong research focus. Pre-PT pathway well-established.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Christiana Care Health System (major Wilmington/Newark regional hospital) — pre-PT clinical access. Philadelphia hospitals 25 min north.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology and Applied Physiology labs. Anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise science all well-covered.
facilityDetails.extras: Philadelphia 25 min by SEPTA. Union (MLS) accessible. Newark College Town — affordable, contained. Delaware beaches 90 min. Washington DC 2 hr. Note: Delaware's July 2025 all-sports move to Conference USA does NOT include men's soccer (CUSA sponsors none) — the Blue Hens compete in the Summit League from 2025.
facilityDetails.note: Whitney Athletic Center is the facility highlight — 10,000 sqft S&C + sports med is strong for a public mid-major. Conference: the July 2025 all-sports move to CUSA excludes men's soccer (CUSA sponsors none); the men's team competes in the Summit League from 2025.

housing.note: Mandatory and guaranteed for all newly admitted first-years the year of admittance; exemption only for living with a parent/guardian within 30 miles, established by May 1. Verified v38 via official udel.edu/students/reslife.

culture.vibe: Classic large public university college town. University of Delaware dominates Newark, DE — it's a college town built around the university. Blue Hen pride is strong. Soccer culture competitive.
culture.thingsToDo: Philadelphia (25 min SEPTA), Delaware beaches (90 min), DC (2 hr). Newark Downtown — bars, restaurants, main street culture. Philadelphia Union MLS.
culture.socialScene: Large public university scene — Greek life, sports culture, college town bars and restaurants on Main Street, Newark.
culture.olivierMatch: Borderline GPA (2.5+). Strong value option for Olivier — low cost, strong kinesiology degree, competitive mid-major D1 soccer, Philly access. Non-warm campus, not city, but good value proposition.
culture.lifestyleTags: College town, low cost, strong kinesiology, Philly proximity, Summit League (MSOC 2025+)

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: Delaware Blue Hens head coach. Strong Applied Physiology program; competitive soccer history (CAA through 2024, Summit League from 2025).
